block_01: It’s been a tough week for the GBP as the UK Pound experienced it’s longest slide against the Euro, since the turn of the century.
block_02: The UK has been battling to keep it’s sterling strong since Brexit was first announced back in June 2016. This year, between the end of February & middle of May, EUR/GBP had managed to find a sidewards range between approximately 0.85 & 0.87, although over the last week, the scales began to tip in favour of the EUR.
block_03: Rising tensions within Theresa May’s government over Brexit, plus continuing tensions between the US & China talks, have no doubt played a role in the recent slide.
block_04: Two days ago the EUR/GBP broke out of the sidewards range it had been in. Will the Euro continue to gain strength against the GBP, or will the sterling, pound its way back to the top?
